name: plaza-civic
summary: A civic plaza — paved, formal, dominated by a single centerpiece (statue, fountain, or obelisk). Benches ring the edges, lanterns mark the paths, and the space is designed to be crossed and observed, not occupied.

accent.purpose: ring or radial-pattern inlay around the centerpiece

primary_palette.note: grand fountain as the plaza's heart
memorial obelisk commemorating a war or founding
clock tower anchors one edge; plaza centers under the chime

story_prompts: execution or proclamation day — crowd gathered, guards forming a ring
quiet afternoon — children throwing coins in the fountain, an old man feeding pigeons
a festival has just ended; benches are broken, the flag is torn, but no one has cleaned up yet
the statue has been defaced overnight with a forbidden sigil
winter at dusk — plaza empty except for a lantern-lighter working the posts

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: outdoor daylight by default; evening 0.35-0.5 with warm lantern tint

anti_patterns: bilateral or radial symmetry is MANDATORY — civic plazas are designed, not grown
invisible walls (iw) at the perimeter — buildings suggested by texture, not modeled
don't clutter — a plaza's power is openness; scatter is minimal
centerpiece MUST be offset from a door axis — visitors should see it from the corner approach
